headline: WebAuthn / passkey attestation verification joins the toolkit as pki.webauthn.
summary: pki.webauthn evaluates a W3C WebAuthn (Level 3) / passkey attestation offline, in pure JavaScript: it parses the attestation object and authenticatorData, decodes the COSE credential public key, and cryptographically verifies each defined attestation-statement format -- packed, tpm, android-key, apple, fido-u2f, and none. parseAttestationObject(bytes) structurally decodes the CBOR attestation object over the strict pki.cbor codec, surfacing the format id, the rpIdHash / flags / signCount, and (for a registration) the attested credential data -- aaguid, credential id, and the decoded COSE public key -- with authenticatorData surfaced as the exact bytes a signature covers. verify(attestationObject, clientDataHash, opts) checks the attestation-statement signature over authenticatorData || clientDataHash and each format's structural bindings: the packed / android-key x5c leaf key, the fido-u2f verificationData, the apple SHA-256 nonce embedded in the leaf certificate, the tpm certInfo magic / type / extraData / Name over the pubArea, and the android-key KeyDescription (attestation challenge, key origin, and sign purpose). Every format binds the attestation certificate's public key to the credential public key with an unsigned-integer comparison, and each x5c leaf's format-specific certificate requirements are enforced (the tpm AIK certificate's tcg-kp-AIKCertificate key purpose and tpmManufacturer / tpmModel / tpmVersion subject alternative name, the packed id-fido-gen-ce-aaguid extension). It composes the strict CBOR and DER codecs, the WebCrypto engine, and the shared certificate-extension decoders -- no new parser -- and fails closed: a malformed shape or a failed check throws a typed WebauthnError (webauthn/*), never a partial verdict. Verified against real attestation objects captured from every one of the five formats (duo-labs/py_webauthn) as an independent oracle. This ships the attestation-statement verifier; chaining the returned trust path to a trusted root -- and resolving a root from an authenticator's aaguid via the FIDO Metadata Service -- is the caller's step via pki.path.validate against roots it pins.

Added: pki.webauthn.parseAttestationObject(bytes) structurally decodes a WebAuthn attestation object (the CBOR { fmt, attStmt, authData }) and its authenticatorData over the strict pki.cbor codec, returning the format id, the decoded rpIdHash / flags / signCount, the attested credential data (aaguid, credentialId, and the decoded COSE credentialPublicKey), and the raw authenticatorData bytes a signature covers. Malformed input throws webauthn/bad-attestation-object. W3C WebAuthn Level 3.

pki.webauthn.verify(attestationObject, clientDataHash, opts) verifies a WebAuthn attestation statement -- packed, tpm, android-key, apple, fido-u2f, or none -- checking the attestation signature over authenticatorData || clientDataHash and each format's structural bindings (the x5c leaf key, the apple nonce, the tpm certInfo Name / extraData over the pubArea, the android KeyDescription, the fido-u2f verificationData), binding each attestation certificate key to the credential public key, and enforcing each format's certificate requirements (a packed leaf's Authenticator Attestation subject and non-CA basic constraints, a tpm AIK's empty subject and non-CA constraints). It resolves the attestation type and trust path or throws a typed webauthn/* error; a signature that does not verify is a webauthn/verify-failed verdict, never a silent pass. The error taxonomy gains WebauthnError (webauthn/*). W3C WebAuthn Level 3, RFC 9052.

The OID registry gains the FIDO id-fido-gen-ce-aaguid, Android key-attestation, Apple anonymous-attestation, and TCG TPM (AIK key purpose, tpmManufacturer / tpmModel / tpmVersion) arcs that WebAuthn attestation certificates carry.
Changed: The bounded big-endian byte cursor under pki.ct's TLS-vector decoding is extracted to a shared engine primitive so the packed big-endian TPM structures in pki.webauthn read through the same bounds-before-slice cursor -- one definition of the length-checked read, carrying each caller's typed error domain.
